skill.name: typegpu
skill.description: TypeGPU and raw WebGPU adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when creating GPU-rendered compositions with TypeGPU, raw WebGPU, WGSL fragment shaders, compute pipelines, liquid glass effects, particle systems, or any canvas layer driven by navigator.gpu that responds to HyperFrames hf-seek events.
skill.summary: Documentation patterns for writing TypeGPU and raw WebGPU compositions that sync to HyperFrames timeline events.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 49 patterns as potentially malicious (32 medium-severity backtick execution, 17 high-severity C2 and crypto keywords), but every flagged location is a markdown code fence, inline code span, WGSL shader snippet, or documentation heading within SKILL.md. The file contains no executable JavaScript, no network calls, no file system access, no environment access, and no command execution. All 49 findings are false positives caused by the static scanner misidentifying markdown backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution and WGSL/GPU terminology as C2 or crypto indicators.

medium_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell backtick execution (32 locations)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown fenced code blocks and inline code spans as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md lines 8, 12, 13, 14, 16, 18, 22, 69, 73, 75, 91, 95, 97, 124, 126, 135, 139, 140, 146, 154, 156, 160, 165, 171, 172, 173. All instances are triple-backtick code fences or single-backtick inline code used to display HTML, JavaScript, and WGSL shader examples for educational documentation. There is no actual shell or Ruby execution in this file.

medium_findings.title: WGSL shader snippets misidentified as C2 keywords (15 locations)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged legitimate WGSL shader code (struct declarations, builtin attributes like @builtin(position) and @location(0), array literals, and vector type names) as C2 keywords at SKILL.md lines 147, 150, 151, 161, 162, 163. These are WGSL grammar tokens for GPU shader programming shown inside a markdown code block. They are not command-and-control indicators; the skill does not perform any network communication.

low_findings.title: Documentation headers and description misidentified as system reconnaissance and weak crypto (4 locations)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and 'System reconnaissance' at SKILL.md:3, 93, 158. Line 3 is the YAML frontmatter description field. Lines 93 and 158 are documentation section headings ('Video-Backed Effects (Liquid Glass, Distortion)' and 'Rounded-Rect SDF (Liquid Glass Pill)'). No cryptography, fingerprinting, or host enumeration occurs in this file.

content.user_title: Build GPU-rendered HyperFrames compositions with TypeGPU and WebGPU
content.value_statement: HyperFrames compositions that need shader-driven effects hit the limits of DOM and CSS. This skill gives Claude the WGSL patterns, WebGPU initialization steps, and timeline integration needed to render GPU frames deterministically across all HyperFrames player modes.
actual_capabilities: Initialize WebGPU asynchronously while registering GSAP timelines synchronously so HyperFrames reads the timeline immediately on load
Render frames in response to the hf-seek custom event using window.__hfTypegpuTime rather than performance.now()
Configure canvas contexts with alphaMode 'opaque' or 'premultiplied' depending on whether the GPU layer is full-frame or overlay
Sample <video> elements into GPU textures via copyExternalImageToTexture for liquid-glass and distortion effects
Generate full-screen triangle vertex shaders in WGSL without a vertex buffer for post-processing passes
Build two-pass downsampled Gaussian blur approximations using bilinear filtering to create frosted glass effects
limitations: Does not generate complete WGSL programs from scratch; provides reference patterns the agent must adapt to the user's pipeline
Cannot execute or test WebGPU code; recommendations are verified by composition behavior in the HyperFrames player
Headless Chrome rendering of video-to-texture copyExternalImageToTexture may fail; production renders may need pre-extracted key frames
Requires the user's environment to expose navigator.gpu; the skill does not polyfill or fall back to WebGL

use_cases.title: Authoring a shader-based overlay composition
use_cases.description: A designer wants particle systems or distortion effects layered on top of an HTML video element using a transparent GPU canvas.

use_cases.title: Migrating an existing WebGL composition to WebGPU
use_cases.description: An engineer has a working WebGL pipeline and wants to convert shaders and render loops to the modern WebGPU API with WGSL.

use_cases.title: Building a glass-morphism or liquid-glass effect
use_cases.description: A creative coder needs a frosted blur, chromatic refraction, and rounded-rect SDF composited on a video input for a brand reveal.
use_cases.target_user: Creative developers producing brand video assets

prompt_templates.title: Create a WebGPU particle overlay
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a HyperFrames composition that uses a transparent WebGPU canvas to render a 5000-particle drift effect over a background video. Use the typegpu skill patterns: register GSAP tweens synchronously, initialize WebGPU inside an async IIFE, listen for hf-seek events, and call onSubmittedWorkDone for render-mode capture.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new GPU overlay composition

prompt_templates.title: Convert a WebGL fragment shader to WGSL
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this WebGL GLSL fragment shader to WGSL, then wire it into a HyperFrames WebGPU canvas using the full-screen triangle vertex shader from the typegpu skill. The shader produces a frosted glass effect over a video texture.

prompt_templates.title: Add a liquid-glass pill to a video
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the rounded-rect SDF and two-pass downsample blur patterns from the typegpu skill, build a HyperFrames composition where a liquid-glass pill with chromatic refraction is composited over a video element. Animate the pill's corner radius from 0 to 32 pixels over 1.5 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a non-deterministic GPU render
prompt_templates.prompt: A HyperFrames composition using the typegpu skill renders correctly in preview but produces different frames each run in render mode. Audit the code for Math.random, requestAnimationFrame, performance.now, and missing onSubmittedWorkDone calls. Suggest specific fixes referencing the determinism section of the typegpu skill.

output_examples.input: How should the WebGPU initialization be ordered relative to the GSAP timeline registration?
output: Step 1: register all GSAP tweens synchronously before any await
Step 2: enter an async IIFE that calls navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() and adapter.requestDevice()
Step 3: configure the canvas context with the device and preferred format
Step 4: add an event listener for hf-seek that calls the render function with e.detail.time
Step 5: for video renders, await device.queue.onSubmittedWorkDone() after each submit

output_examples.input: What alphaMode should I use for a particle overlay above a regular video element?
output: Use alphaMode: 'premultiplied' on the GPU canvas context configuration
The GPU canvas remains transparent where alpha is 0, letting the underlying <video> show through
Reserve alphaMode: 'opaque' for cases where the GPU pipeline paints the full frame including the video content

best_practices: Register all GSAP timelines and HF timeline handles synchronously before the first await, so the HyperFrames player reads them at page load.
Drive every GPU render from the hf-seek event and window.__hfTypegpuTime, never from requestAnimationFrame or performance.now, to keep compositions deterministic across player and render modes.
For video-backed GPU effects, wait for loadedmetadata before creating the source texture and use the video's native dimensions to avoid scaling artifacts.
anti_patterns: Do not call Math.random() inside render or shader code; use a seeded PRNG so frames are reproducible across runs.
Do not use requestAnimationFrame as the render loop; render only in response to hf-seek events to match HyperFrames timeline scrubbing.
Do not rely on copyExternalImageToTexture for video inputs in headless render mode; pre-extract key frames as PNGs for production renders.

faq.question: Does this skill run any code itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a single markdown file containing documentation, WGSL snippets, and reference patterns. The agent reads it and writes new composition code for the user.

faq.question: What WebGPU feature is required?
faq.answer: The host browser must expose navigator.gpu and successfully return an adapter from navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(). The skill does not provide a WebGL fallback.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with the standard HyperFrames video element?
faq.answer: Yes. Use alphaMode: 'premultiplied' to make the GPU canvas transparent and let the video element beneath it show through, then use copyExternalImageToTexture to feed the video into a shader.

faq.question: Why must GSAP tweens be registered before any await?
faq.answer: The HyperFrames player reads window.__timelines immediately on page load. If you register inside an async block, the player may have already scanned the page and missed the timeline.

faq.question: How do I ensure render-mode output matches preview output?
faq.answer: Avoid Math.random, requestAnimationFrame, and performance.now. Render only on hf-seek events. After each GPU submit, await device.queue.onSubmittedWorkDone() so the frame is flushed before capture.

faq.question: What is the recommended way to create frosted-glass blur in WGSL?
faq.answer: Use a two-pass approach: first render the full-resolution texture to a 1/6 scale texture, then sample that small texture with bilinear filtering for the frosted interior while keeping full-resolution sampling for sharp areas and chromatic refraction.
